title: Continuous Delivery Outcomes, Flow, Feedback, and Evidence · CI/CD Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for continuous delivery outcomes, flow, feedback, and evidence and requires the learner to defend the smallest safe delivery outcome and evidence map. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for delivery outcome and evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.
Relate batch size, queue time, feedback latency, failure isolation, recovery cost, and learning speed without treating deployment frequency as the objective.
Trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries delivery-system evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries, keep Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A quiz service deploys 4 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric. Competency probe: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A quiz service deploys 4 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric. Competency probe: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Continuous delivery means every successful commit must immediately receive production traffic.

options.text: Capture event, workflow and revision identity, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, and release state for the quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries case
Predict expression, shell, graph, trust, cache, artifact, deployment, user-signal, failure, and cleanup effects before mutation for the quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries case
Review the smallest workflow, policy, build, attestation, or release increment in the deterministic browser simulator for the quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries case
Inject one fault, record causal evidence, repair and rollback, then name authorized disposable delivery-environment transfer evidence for the quiz Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries case

stimulus.title: Ci Release Hypothesis Done failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Pipeline completion is the same as user-value completion.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define an observable release hypothesis, risk budget, evidence owner, success and abort signals, and done condition before automating.

options.text: Run the Ci Release Hypothesis Done boundary case for delivery outcome and evidence map, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Ci Release Hypothesis Done result because visible delivery outcome and evidence map output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Ci Release Hypothesis Done.

For diagnose the hidden failure, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof. This does not satisfy Ci Release Hypothesis Done for delivery outcome and evidence map.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define an observable release hypothesis, risk budget, evidence owner, success and abort signals, and done condition before automating.
